label: Conditional Random Field
definition: A probabilistic model for sequence labeling that predicts an entire label sequence jointly, modeling the dependencies between adjacent labels, which made it the dominant pre-neural method for tasks like named-entity recognition and part-of-speech tagging.

body.text: A conditional random field labels a sequence by considering the whole sequence at once. Tagging each token independently ignores a basic fact: labels constrain their neighbors. In the common BIO scheme for named entities, an "inside" tag cannot follow an "outside" tag, and a CRF captures exactly these label-to-label transition rules, scoring an entire candidate label sequence rather than each position alone and choosing the best consistent one.

It is best understood against the hidden Markov model it largely replaced. An HMM is generative and makes strong independence assumptions about how observations are produced. A CRF is discriminative: it conditions directly on the entire input sequence and can use rich, overlapping features of the words without worrying about modeling their distribution, which made it both more accurate and more flexible. For roughly a decade it was the state of the art for sequence-labeling tasks across NLP.

Even after deep learning arrived, the CRF did not simply vanish. A very common architecture put a CRF layer on top of a neural encoder, the BiLSTM-CRF, letting the network learn features while the CRF enforced valid label transitions, combining learned representations with structured output.

Understanding it illuminates a general principle that still matters: when outputs are structured and interdependent, modeling the structure of the output, not just classifying each piece in isolation, is what produces coherent results, an idea that echoes in constrained and structured decoding for modern language models.

image.alt: A trellis grid with five columns for time steps t=1 to t=5 and five rows for possible states i=1 to i=5, every cell a circle. Most circles are gray, and one circle per column is ringed and joined by a bold line into a single path that zigzags across the columns.
image.caption: Sequence labeling can be drawn as a trellis: at each position the token could take any of several labels (the rows), and the bold path is the one assignment chosen for the whole sequence. A conditional random field scores entire label paths rather than each position alone, using the transitions between neighboring labels, and selects the single best consistent path through the lattice.
